mnemonics: MEET DBMS: Manage, Enter, Enter, Track, Database
DBMS ASSISTANT: Data By Many Systems
keyPoints: Introduction to Databases is a branch of computer science that deals with the systematic storage and manipulation of data.
Characteristics of database approach: Data independence, Vertical and horizontal scaling, Data redundancy, Data consistency.
Advantages of using the DBMS approach: Improved data security, Reduced data redundancy, Improved data consistency, Simplified data retrieval.

History of database applications: 1960s-1970s: First database management systems were developed, 1980s-1990s: Relational databases became popular, 2000s-present: Object-oriented and NoSQL databases emerged.
